task_completion.description: Verify the sample order lines dataset is loaded into Oracle Analytics Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data Elements pane on the left shows 'sample_order_lines2023' as the active dataset.

task_completion.description: Verify a calculated column named 'Day Type' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A data element named 'Day Type' is visible in the Data Elements pane, typically with an icon indicating it is a custom calculation.

task_completion.description: Verify the visualization type is set to a Donut Chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main canvas displays a Donut Chart (a pie chart with a hollow center), or the Donut Chart icon is selected in the visualization type menu.

task_completion.description: Verify the chart visualizes 'Sales' by 'Day Type' with the correct labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Grammar pane shows 'Sales' in the Values section and 'Day Type' in the Category/Color section. The chart legend or data labels explicitly display the text 'Weekend' and 'Weekday'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Donut Chart is correctly titled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title text 'Revenue Share: Weekend vs Weekday' is clearly visible at the top of the Donut Chart visualization.

task_completion.description: Verify the workbook is saved with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top title bar or active tab of Oracle Analytics Desktop displays 'Weekend_Analysis', or a File Explorer window shows 'Weekend_Analysis.dva' in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents' folder.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual Oracle Analytics Desktop application interface being manipulated, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the visualization is genuinely rendered by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Donut Chart is rendered inside the Oracle Analytics canvas with standard UI elements (Grammar pane, Data pane) surrounding it, not an image opened in a photo viewer or browser.

integrity.description: Verify the labels are data-driven and not static text boxes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Weekend' and 'Weekday' labels appear in the official chart legend or as data-bound labels, rather than being manually added as floating text boxes over the canvas.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dataset path is 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\OracleAnalyticsData\sample_order_lines2023.xlsx'. The visualization must be a Donut Chart titled exactly 'Revenue Share: Weekend vs Weekday' and must include the calculated labels 'Weekend' and 'Weekday'. The final workbook must be saved as 'Weekend_Analysis.dva'.
